metadata.name: ww-video-weweb
metadata.description: A native HTML video player for videos uploaded to or served by WeWeb, with poster image, playback controls, events, actions, and playback state variables.
features: Autoplay, muted, loop, controls, and preload settings
Playback events for play, pause, and end
Programmatic play, pause, and seek actions
Read-only variables for playing state and current time

properties.name: file
properties.description: The video file URL or WeWeb video asset path to play.

properties.name: previewImage
properties.description: Poster image URL or WeWeb image asset path displayed before playback.

properties.name: videoStartTime
properties.description: Initial playback position in seconds. Always include this property and use 0 by default; omitting it makes the video fail during mount.

properties.name: autoplay
properties.description: Whether the video should automatically start playing outside editor mode. Autoplay generally requires muted to be true in browsers.

properties.name: muted
properties.description: Whether the video audio is muted.

properties.name: loop
properties.description: Whether the video should restart automatically when it reaches the end.

properties.name: controls
properties.description: Whether native browser video controls are displayed.

properties.name: preload
properties.description: Whether the browser should preload video data.

events.name: play
events.description: Triggered when playback starts or resumes.

events.name: end
events.description: Triggered when playback reaches the end.

actions.label: Seek to
actions.description: Move playback to a specific time in seconds.

variables.name: Is Playing
variables.description: Whether the video is currently playing.

specifications: Use this element for videos uploaded to or served by WeWeb. For YouTube, Vimeo, Twitch, or Dailymotion embeds, use their dedicated video elements instead.
Set file to a valid video URL or WeWeb video asset path; the element renders no useful media without it.
Use previewImage when a poster image should appear before playback.
Always include videoStartTime, even when the video should start at the beginning. Use 0 by default. The component expects a finite number during mount.
When autoplay is true, set muted to true to satisfy browser autoplay restrictions.
The video fills its element box, so set an explicit height or aspectRatio in style for predictable layout.
The play, pause, and end events expose the current playback time under event.value.
Use the playVideo, pauseVideo, and seekTo actions for workflow-driven playback control.

examples.description: Autoplaying muted hero video with a poster image.
